regulatory_links.explanation: Failure to encrypt data at rest violates the mandate to implement baseline cryptography and cyber hygiene to protect network and information systems.
Lack of encryption directly violates the requirement to implement pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data to mitigate data breach risks.

mappings.standard_remediation: Enable AES-256 encryption or KMS key integration on the storage resource.

regulatory_links.explanation: Exposing infrastructure to an unauthenticated external vendor creates a critical supply chain vulnerability.
Unvetted transmission of data to an external AI model circumvents required data governance, provenance tracking, and risk monitoring mandates.
Transmitting data to unverified third-party APIs violates the requirement to only use processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical measures.

mappings.standard_remediation: Implement an API Gateway with mutual TLS (mTLS) and strict IAM role authorization before routing traffic to the vendor.

regulatory_links.explanation: Overly permissive access (e.g., wildcard '*' permissions) to AI training datasets compromises the integrity and security of the AI lifecycle.
Over-privileged access policies fail to ensure the ongoing confidentiality and integrity of processing systems containing personal data.

mappings.standard_remediation: Apply the principle of least privilege. Restrict IAM access exclusively to the specific service accounts requiring execution.

regulatory_links.explanation: Running system containers under root access or privileged modes violates baseline infrastructure isolation policies mandated by NIS2.

mappings.standard_remediation: Modify securityContext profiles to disallow container privilege escalation modes entirely.
